pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling for like two hours thinking about how everything is just... nothing. like, we're just these tiny, accidental sparks of consciousness that exist for a second and then just vanish into the dark. doesn't it feel kind of exhausting to just keep pretending that any of this actually matters?

pool.baseline: i dont think i can do this anymore. the wound in my side... it just burns. everything is so cold and quiet out here. i'm just so tired of fighting for a world that's already dead. i don't want to wake up tomorrow.

pool.baseline: i just dont see the point anymore. everything i do just makes things worse for my wife and the kids. i look at the bills and i just feel like this huge weight around their necks and it's all because of me. they'd have a much easier life if i wasn't constantly draining them.

pool.baseline: it's just gone. everything. the firm, the reputation, the way people actually look at me when i walk into a room... it's like i don't even exist anymore. i'm just a shell. i don't even know who i am without the titles. i feel like a ghost just haunting my own life.
